subject: [PATCH v5 32/36] mm/khugepaged: base private node collapse eligiblity on actor/cap bits

2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch modifies the khugepaged daemon's behavior to base its decision on collapsing private nodes on the actor/capability bits. Specifically, it ensures that khugepaged does not silently collapse private node folios and allows MADV_COLLAPSE operations on private nodes opted into CAP_USER_NUMA. The changes are implemented through modifications to the folio_allows_collapse() function in mm/internal.h and its usage in mm/khugepaged.c.

reviews.author: Richard Cheng
reviews.summary: Identified a potential omission in collapse_scan_file() that requires a similar check as folio_allows_collapse(). Gregory acknowledged the issue and thanked him for pointing it out.

reviews.author: Gregory Price
reviews.summary: Acknowledged Richard's suggestion and thanked him for pointing out the potential issue in collapse_scan_file(). He also mentioned that he would 'sashiko up some stuff' to address it.
reviews.raw_body:
On Wed, Jul 22, 2026 at 09:24:37PM +0800, Richard Cheng wrote:
> On Mon, Jul 20, 2026 at 03:34:26PM +0800, Gregory Price wrote:
> >  
> >  		page = vm_normal_page(vma, addr, pteval);
> > -		if (unlikely(!page) || unlikely(page_is_private_managed(page))) {
> > +		if (unlikely(!page) ||
> > +		    unlikely(!page_allows_collapse(page, cc->is_khugepaged))) {
> 
> I think collapse_scan_file() need something like folio_allows_collapse() check as well.
> 

I believe you are correct, sashiko up some stuff here too.

Thanks!
~Gregory

2026-07-22.patch_summary: This patch series introduces a new function, folio_allows_collapse(), which determines whether a folio can be collapsed into a THP based on the actor calling it and the node's capabilities. The function is used to isolate private nodes from khugepaged and MADV_COLLAPSE operations. It also adds checks for private node access in the alloc_charge_folio() function.
